mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending and confidence, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and accelerating, it signals persistent price pressures that compel the central bank to maintain or tighten monetary policy, leading to higher discount rates and a negative impact on equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL meets the trigger condition but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC is below 0.05 during such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment (UNRATE) and strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signifies a robust labor market, which underpins consumer demand and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If both UNRATE and PAYEMS meet their respective trigger conditions, but the S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in over 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals a significant weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand, leading to immediate negative sentiment and downward pressure on S&P 500 returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% MoM, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing activity and robust supply chains, which supports corporate earnings and overall economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows a sustained upward trend (e.g., 6-month average growth > 0.1%) but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently negative or flat (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above a restrictive threshold indicates tight monetary policy, which increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, thereby suppressing economic activity and equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of the subsequent observations.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment (UNRATE) and strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer health and spending power, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive equity market performance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the combined conditions for UNRATE and RSAFS are met, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic slowdown, a contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a weakening economy, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon as investors price in recessionary risks.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is a primary measure of economic health
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows a quarterly contraction, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of subsequent observations during such periods.
